description: Go daemon changes to consume new activity classification and workflow state predictions from sigil-ml, generate LLM-driven suggestions, and write feedback events for continuous learning.

userStories.title: Update MCP tool known model list
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the get_predictions MCP tool to query the new model names so the LLM can read activity classifications and workflow state assessments.
acceptanceCriteria: In internal/mcp/tools.go, the get_predictions tool queries models: activity, suggest, quality, stuck, duration
When no specific model is requested, all 5 models are queried and returned
Existing get_predictions behavior for specific model queries is unchanged
go build ./... passes
go vet ./... passes
userStories.notes: File: internal/mcp/tools.go around line 107. Change the models slice from the old list to the new one.

userStories.title: Add get_workflow_state MCP tool
userStories.description: As the LLM, I need a dedicated tool to fetch the current workflow state so I can understand flow state probabilities, momentum, and focus before generating suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: New tool 'get_workflow_state' registered in internal/mcp/tools.go via RegisterStoreTools
Tool description explains it returns flow state probabilities (deep_work, shallow_work, exploring, blocked, winding_down), momentum, focus score, and activity breakdown
Tool queries store.QueryLatestPrediction(ctx, 'suggest') and returns the full result JSON
Returns a helpful message if no prediction exists yet
Tool takes no parameters
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: File: internal/mcp/tools.go. Follow the existing tool registration pattern. The suggest model now contains workflow state, not just an action string.

userStories.title: Add get_activity_stream MCP tool
userStories.description: As the LLM, I need a tool to see the recent classified activity stream so I can understand what the engineer has been doing in the last few minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: New tool 'get_activity_stream' registered in internal/mcp/tools.go
Tool description explains it returns recent events classified by activity type (creating, refining, verifying, navigating, researching, integrating, communicating, idle)
Tool queries store.QueryLatestPrediction(ctx, 'activity') and returns the classified event summary
Returns a helpful message if no activity predictions exist yet
Tool takes no parameters
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: File: internal/mcp/tools.go. The activity model writes classified event summaries to ml_predictions with model='activity'.

userStories.title: Update MCP system prompt for workflow-aware suggestions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LLM system prompt to instruct the model to use workflow state predictions before generating suggestions.
acceptanceCriteria: System prompt in internal/mcp/loop.go is updated to instruct the LLM to call get_workflow_state before making suggestions
Prompt includes guidance: don't interrupt deep_work, help with blocked state, be specific using activity stream context
Prompt instructs concise one-sentence suggestions
Existing tool loop behavior is unchanged (max rounds, fallback)
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: File: internal/mcp/loop.go, the systemPrompt constant. Keep it concise — the LLM reads this on every query.

userStories.title: Write ml_feedback events on suggestion accept/dismiss
userStories.description: As the ML system, I need feedback events written when users accept or dismiss suggestions so sigil-ml can learn from outcomes.
acceptanceCriteria: When a suggestion is accepted, an event with kind='ml_feedback' is inserted into the events table
When a suggestion is dismissed, an event with kind='ml_feedback' and accepted=false is inserted
Payload includes: model name, accepted boolean, and current workflow state from latest prediction
Feedback events are written in the existing feedback handler in internal/notifier/notifier.go or cmd/sigild/main.go feedback handler
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: The feedback socket handler already exists (srv.Handle 'feedback' in main.go). Add an InsertEvent call there alongside the existing InsertFeedback call.

userStories.title: Add sigilctl correct command for classification corrections
userStories.description: As a user, I want to correct misclassified events so the ActivityClassifier can learn from my corrections.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'correct' command added to cmd/sigilctl/main.go switch statement
Usage: sigilctl correct <event_id> <category>
Writes an event with kind='ml_correction' to the events table via the socket
Payload includes event_id (int) and correct_category (string)
Valid categories: creating, refining, verifying, navigating, researching, integrating, communicating, idle
Returns error for invalid category names
New socket handler 'correct' in cmd/sigild/main.go that writes the event
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: Add both the sigilctl command and the socket handler. The correction event is the highest-signal feedback for the ActivityClassifier.

userStories.title: Task tracker respects flow state from predictions
userStories.description: As a developer, I don't want to be interrupted during deep work, and I want more help when I'm blocked.
acceptanceCriteria: Task tracker reads latest workflow state prediction before firing OnTransition callback
When dominant_state is 'deep_work' and focus_score > 0.8, the transition callback is skipped (no LLM suggestion generated)
When dominant_state is 'blocked' and momentum < -0.5, the suggestion confidence is elevated to ConfidenceStrong
Workflow state is read via the existing mlPredictor.Predict interface or directly from store.QueryLatestPrediction
If no workflow prediction exists, behavior is unchanged (fallback to current logic)
go build ./... passes
userStories.notes: File: internal/task/tracker.go and cmd/sigild/main.go (OnTransition callback). Read the suggest prediction, parse dominant_state and focus_score from the result JSON.
